skill.name: firebase-ai-logic
skill.description: Official skill for integrating Firebase AI Logic (Gemini API) into web applications. Covers setup, multimodal inference, structured output, and security.
skill.summary: Integrate Google Gemini AI into web apps using Firebase AI Logic SDK with setup, text/image generation, chat, and streaming support.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: Skill reviewed and found to be legitimate Firebase product documentation. Static scanner generated false positives: markdown code formatting (backticks) was misidentified as Ruby command injection, Firebase documentation URLs flagged as network exfiltration, and YAML front matter flagged as weak cryptography. No malicious code, credential theft, or harmful patterns detected.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 26 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at SKILL.md and usage_patterns_web.md. These are markdown code formatting delimiters (backticks), NOT Ruby backtick command execution. The actual content uses standard npm/npx commands for Firebase CLI tool installation, which is legitimate package management.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Content clearly shows markdown code blocks with standard CLI commands like 'npm install -g firebase@latest' - no code injection or shell execution present

dangerous_patterns.title: Firebase Documentation URLs Misidentified as Network Exfiltration
dangerous_patterns.description: Static scanner flagged 5 'Hardcoded URL' instances. These are legitimate Firebase documentation links hardcoded in markdown documentation. No credential exfiltration or unauthorized network requests exist.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to firebase.google.com documentation domains - legitimate documentation references, not external data transmission

content.user_title: Integrate Gemini AI into Firebase web apps
content.value_statement: Adding AI capabilities to web applications requires understanding Firebase SDK setup and Gemini API integration. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for integrating generative AI with text, images, chat, and streaming support.
actual_capabilities: Initialize Firebase AI Logic SDK with Gemini Developer API or Vertex AI
Generate text responses using generateContent with text prompts
Analyze images, audio, video, and PDF files using multimodal inputs
Create multi-turn chat sessions with automatic history using startChat
Stream partial results for real-time UI updates using generateContentStream
Generate structured JSON output using schema validation
limitations: Does not handle backend server setup - requires Firebase project configuration
API key security depends on implementing Firebase App Check
File uploads over 20MB require Cloud Storage for Firebase instead of inline data

use_cases.title: Build AI-powered product search
use_cases.description: Add multimodal search where users upload images to find similar products. Gemini analyzes the image and returns matching product descriptions.

use_cases.title: Create conversational customer support
use_cases.description: Implement chat-based support with multi-turn conversation history. The AI remembers context across messages for natural dialogue.

use_cases.title: Generate image captions for uploads
use_cases.description: Automatically generate alt text and captions for user-uploaded images. Useful for accessibility and content management.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize Firebase AI project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Firebase AI Logic in my web project. I have Node.js installed and want to use the Gemini Developer API. Guide me through the Firebase setup steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up a new Firebase project with AI Logic

prompt_templates.title: Add image analysis to app
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to add image analysis to my web app using Firebase AI Logic. Show me how to upload an image and get AI-generated descriptions or classification.

prompt_templates.title: Implement streaming chat
prompt_templates.prompt: My users want real-time AI responses like a typing effect. Show me how to use streaming responses with Firebase AI Logic for better UX.

prompt_templates.title: Configure secure API access
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to prevent unauthorized API usage. Guide me through setting up Firebase App Check and Remote Config for secure model management.

output_examples.input: User asks to explain how to analyze an uploaded image with AI
output_examples.output: Use the fileToGenerativePart helper to convert the image to base64, then pass it with your prompt to generateContent. The AI will analyze the image and return descriptions or answer questions about it.

output_examples.input: User asks for real-time typing effect responses
output_examples.output: Replace generateContent with generateContentStream. Iterate through the stream chunks to display partial results as they arrive, creating a typing animation effect in your UI.

best_practices: Always initialize the AI service after the main Firebase App instance to ensure proper configuration order
Use Firebase App Check to protect your API quota from unauthorized client access in production
Use gemini-flash-latest by default for best performance, and only choose specific models when requirements demand it
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode model names like 'gemini-1.5-flash' - use Remote Config to update model versions without deploying new code
Do not skip Firebase App Check in production - this exposes your API to quota theft
Do not pass files over 20MB as inline data - use Cloud Storage for Firebase URLs instead to avoid HTTP 413 errors

faq.question: What is the difference between Gemini Developer API and Vertex AI?
faq.answer: Gemini Developer API has a free tier for prototyping with pay-as-you-go pricing. Vertex AI requires Blaze plan but offers enterprise-grade scalability and compliance features.

faq.question: How do I handle large file uploads?
faq.answer: Files over 20MB should be stored in Cloud Storage for Firebase and passed as URLs to the Gemini API. This prevents HTTP 413 errors from inline data size limits.

faq.question: How do I protect my API from unauthorized usage?
faq.answer: Enable Firebase App Check with reCAPTCHA Enterprise provider. This verifies that requests come from your legitimate app, preventing quota theft.

faq.question: Can I use Firebase AI Logic for on-device inference?
faq.answer: Yes, hybrid on-device inference is available for web apps. The SDK automatically checks for Gemini Nano availability and switches between local or cloud execution.

faq.question: How do I update the model without deploying new code?
faq.answer: Use Firebase Remote Config to store model names. This lets you change models dynamically by updating configuration values in the Firebase console.

faq.question: What frameworks does Firebase AI Logic support?
faq.answer: Firebase AI Logic supports web (JavaScript), Android (Kotlin/Java), iOS (Swift), Flutter (Dart), and Unity (C#) through their respective SDKs.
